pattern: A recursive-descent parse over the formula maintains a multiplier stack so parenthesised groups scale their inner atom counts, then names are emitted in sorted order.
hints: Walk the string left to right, reading an element name as one uppercase letter followed by lowercase letters, then an optional number.
Parentheses nest, so a stack of count maps (or a multiplier stack) lets a group's trailing number scale everything inside it.
On '(' push a fresh scope, on ')' read the following number and merge the popped scope's counts times that number into the parent, then sort the final names and append counts greater than one.

edge_inputs.description: nested group scaled by a trailing number
single element of count one emits no number
two-letter element name and counts of one
same element appears in multiple groups and must accumulate

stress.description: deterministically build a balanced nested formula of single-letter elements with counts
stress.note: valid formulas only, single uppercase letters keep names unambiguous
